description: Comprehensive operational dashboard for the Vorion AI Governance Platform. Monitors trust engine, AI gateway routing, intent pipeline, proof plane, circuit breakers, and SLA compliance.

panels.description: Distribution of agent trust scores across the T0-T7 tier spectrum. Score range: 0 (Untrusted) to 1000 (Autonomous).
panels.title: Trust Score Distribution

panels.description: Rate of trust tier transitions. Upward transitions indicate growing trust; downward transitions may indicate policy violations or trust decay.
panels.title: Trust Tier Transitions

panels.description: Trust decay rate tracking. The TrustFacade applies asymmetric decay -- failures are weighted more heavily (0.5) vs successes (0.1). Observation ceiling caps from BLACK_BOX (500), GRAY_BOX (800), WHITE_BOX (1000).
panels.title: Trust Decay / Accrual Rates

panels.description: Requests routed by the AI Gateway per routing strategy: privacy (Ollama/self-hosted), specialized (task-fit), green (carbon-aware), cost-optimized (cascade).
panels.title: Routing Distribution

panels.description: Gateway latency percentiles. SLA targets from sla-tracker.ts: p50 < 1000ms, p95 < 3000ms, p99 < 5000ms.
panels.title: Gateway Latency (p50 / p95 / p99)

panels.description: Per-provider request rates across the gateway. Providers: anthropic, google, ollama, openai, azure, bedrock (from health-checker.ts ProviderId).
panels.title: Model Usage by Provider

panels.description: Degraded mode percentage from DegradedTracker. Tracks when the gateway serves fallback/degraded responses instead of primary model responses.
panels.title: Degraded Mode %

panels.description: Fallback invocations by model. From DegradedTracker.fallbacksByModel -- tracks which fallback models are absorbing load when primary models fail.
panels.title: Fallback Invocations by Model

panels.description: Token consumption by provider and model. Quota tiers from quota-manager.ts: free (50K/day), starter (500K/day), professional (5M/day), enterprise (50M/day).
panels.title: Token Throughput by Provider

panels.description: Intent throughput from IntentPipeline.getMetrics(). Tracks totalIntents, allowedIntents, deniedIntents.
panels.title: Intent Throughput

panels.description: Ratio of allowed vs denied intents. The pipeline flow is: Intent -> Gate Check -> Authorization -> Execution -> Proof. Denials can come from gate admission or authorization.
panels.title: Allow / Deny Ratio

panels.description: Intent processing latency from IntentPipeline.submit(). Tracks the full pipeline: gate check + authorization + execution + proof commit.
panels.title: Intent Processing Latency

panels.description: Escalation rate: percentage of intents requiring human intervention or elevated review. Tracks gate denials that trigger human-gateway (from council/agents/human-gateway).
panels.title: Escalation Rate

panels.description: Proof commitment rate from ProofCommitter. The HOT PATH commit() targets <1ms latency. Commitments include: intent_submitted, decision_made, execution_started, execution_completed.
panels.title: Proof Commit Rate

panels.description: Merkle tree batch processing. ProofCommitter builds Merkle trees from SHA-256 hashes during batch flush. Batch integrity is verified via merkle root comparison.
panels.title: Batch Processing & Flush Latency

panels.description: Proof buffer size and batch sizes. The ProofCommitter buffer is flushed when it reaches maxBufferSize or on the flushIntervalMs timer. Large buffers indicate backpressure.
panels.title: Proof Buffer & Batch Sizes

panels.description: Chain integrity verification. Tracks hash chain continuity and Merkle proof verification results. Any verification failure indicates potential tampering.
panels.title: Chain Integrity Failures

panels.description: Commitment latency from ProofCommitter.commit() - the synchronous HOT PATH. Target is <1ms per commit. Warnings are logged for any commit exceeding 1ms.
panels.title: Commit Latency Heatmap

panels.description: Per-provider circuit breaker state timeline. States: closed (healthy), half-open (testing recovery), open (blocking requests). Config defaults: failureThreshold=5, resetTimeout=30s, successThreshold=3.
panels.title: Circuit Breaker State (per Provider)

panels.description: Failure rates per provider from CircuitBreaker.getCircuitMetrics(). Tracks failureRate, requestsBlocked, requestsAllowed per circuit.
panels.title: Provider Failure Rates

panels.description: Circuit breaker trip and recovery events. Trips (circuit opening) indicate provider failures reaching threshold. Recoveries (circuit closing after half-open) indicate provider restored.
panels.title: Circuit Trip & Recovery Events

panels.description: Requests blocked vs allowed by circuit breakers. High blocked counts indicate providers under stress or circuit breakers protecting the system from cascading failures.
panels.title: Requests Blocked vs Allowed

panels.description: Average circuit recovery time from CircuitMetrics.averageRecoveryTimeMs. Measured from last failure to circuit closure.
panels.title: Circuit Recovery Time

panels.description: Provider health status from HealthChecker. Tracks health status, success rate, and latency for each provider. Providers are checked every 30s by default.
panels.title: Provider Health Summary

title: Vorion Platform Overview
